title: Decision Trees Mapped to Memristive Hardware for Network Intrusion Detection
abstract: The increasing need for real-time, interpretable network intrusion detection at the edge drives the demand for novel lightweight hardware implementations suited for resource-constrained environments. In this work, we investigate decision trees trained on ACI-IoT-2023 and CIC-IDS-2017 datasets and map them onto an emerging hardware architecture, leveraging a custom mixed-signal memristor prototyping platform. More precisely, we map decision node thresholds and boundary conditions onto memristive devices through a conductance encoding scheme. Simulations using a digital twin of our hardware confirm behavioral parity with software models, while experimental results from the physical memristive arrays demonstrate successful deployment of decision trees on hardware. For a lightweight 9-node decision tree trained on the ACI-IoT-2023 dataset, we achieve 87% classification accuracy on 3000 test samples, matching results of the software implementation with negligible impact due to memristor variations. This study establishes an interpretable framework for network intrusion detection on novel hardware using memristive devices.
